questions.text: An expression containing only one term is called a:
questions.explanation: Monomials are algebraic expressions with a single non-zero term.

questions.text: The numerical factor in a term is called its:
questions.explanation: In the term 5xy, 5 is the numerical coefficient.

questions.text: Terms which have the same algebraic factors are called:
questions.explanation: Like terms have the same variables raised to the same powers.

questions.text: What is the result of adding 3x and 4x?
questions.explanation: When adding like terms, we add their numerical coefficients: (3+4)x = 7x.

questions.text: The value of x² - 5 when x = 3 is:
Which of the following is a binomial?
questions.explanation: A binomial is an expression containing exactly two terms.

questions.text: What are the terms in the expression 4x - 3y?
questions.explanation: Terms are added to form expressions; here the terms are 4x and (-3y).

questions.text: The degree of the constant term 7 is:
questions.explanation: A constant can be written as 7x⁰, so its degree is 0.
